subtitle: Medial ankle pain — deltoid ligament or tibialis posterior

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Medial ankle pain often linked to overpronation and high mileage

mild.description: Inner ankle ache after runs, particularly on longer efforts
mild.can_run_note: Yes — check footwear for signs of overpronation
why: Tibialis posterior tendinopathy from overpronation overloading the medial arch
Medial ankle stress from excessive foot roll-in during stance phase
Flat feet increasing medial ankle load
relief: Arch support or stability shoe to reduce medial load
Tibialis posterior strengthening: heel raises with arch lift
Short foot exercise to build intrinsic foot muscle support
prevention: Avoid sudden increase in mileage on hard surfaces

moderate.description: Medial ankle pain during runs, tender to press behind inner ankle bone
moderate.can_run_note: Easy flat runs only — avoid any lateral or uneven surfaces
why: Tibialis posterior tendinopathy progressing without intervention
Possible spring ligament stress from chronic overpronation
Medial ankle impingement in hypermobile athletes
relief: Arch support in all footwear including around the house
Eccentric tibialis posterior loading: slow single-leg heel raises
prevention: Foot strengthening to support the medial arch long-term

severe.description: Significant medial ankle pain, arch collapse, unable to run
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — tibialis posterior insufficiency can lead to flat foot deformity
why: Tibialis posterior tendon tear or rupture — progressive flat foot
Deltoid ligament sprain from medial ankle sprain
relief: Immobilisation boot if significant tendon involvement
prevention: Full rehabilitation and orthotic support before return to running
Lifelong foot strengthening to prevent recurrence

see_physio_if: Visible flattening of the arch that has changed over time
No improvement after 2 weeks of arch support and rest
